question_text: Two identical containers are filled with different liquids. Container A has a mass of 250 g and Container B has a mass of 200 g. Both containers have the same volume. Which liquid has the greater density?

options.label: A
options.text: Container A's liquid has greater density

options.label: B
options.text: Container B's liquid has greater density

options.label: C
options.text: Both liquids have the same density

options.label: D
options.text: We cannot tell without knowing the volume

explanation: Since both containers have the same volume, the one with greater mass must have greater density. Container A has more mass (250 g vs 200 g), so its liquid is denser. Density only depends on mass and volume, which we know for both.
